message:
fix(core): harden btrfs hard-link and dir-entry removal correctness

Prevent hard-link creation when the destination name already exists in
the parent directory by adding an early btrfs_lookup_dir_entry guard
that returns FfsError::Exists, matching POSIX EEXIST semantics.

Rewrite btrfs_remove_dir_entry to propagate errors instead of silently
swallowing them with warn!() logs.  Both DIR_ITEM and DIR_INDEX
deletions now fail with FfsError::Corruption when the expected name is
absent, preventing silent desynchronisation of dir items, inode refs,
and nlink state.

Remove the now-unnecessary clippy::unnecessary_wraps allow attribute
since the function legitimately returns Result after this change.

Add two new tests:
- btrfs_remove_dir_entry_missing_name_reports_corruption: verifies that
  removing a non-existent name is treated as corruption.
- btrfs_write_hard_link_existing_name_returns_exists: verifies that
  linking over an occupied name returns Exists and leaves both inodes
  intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
